repository.name: ot-tag-registry
repository.description: [DEMO] Industrial sensor tags are usually a mess of confusing spreadsheets, causing mixed-up names and super slow setups. The OT Tag Registry fixes this by giving you one central hub to easily create, organize, and standardize all your tags, keeping everything totally consistent and way easier to manage everywhere.

commit.message:
fix(services): improve agent prompt for tag name generation

Use short codes instead of display names in tag name construction.
Add tool-failure fallback mappings for site/line resolution.
Always copy unit/datatype from search results unless the query
explicitly describes a different measurement.

fix(services): use account-level endpoint for embedding clients

Embeddings are deployed at the account scope, not the project scope.
Use AI_SERVICES_ENDPOINT (falling back to stripping PROJECT_ENDPOINT)
in both auto_fill.py and seed_index.py.

commit.message:
fix(server): separate account-level and project-level AI clients

Split _get_ai_client into _get_ai_client (account-level endpoint
for embeddings) and _get_agent_client (project-scoped for agent
calls). Fall back to stripping PROJECT_ENDPOINT when
AI_SERVICES_ENDPOINT is unset. Increase tool-call timeouts to 30s.

commit.message:
fix(infra): add retry logic for agent setup in postprovision

AI Foundry projects may take time to propagate after fresh creation.
Retry agent setup up to 3 times with 30s delays, and warn instead
of failing if all attempts are exhausted.

commit.message:
fix: convert tempo sessions to recovery when NFI is red and fatigued

The tempo case in generateWorkout() was missing the red-NFI recovery
guard present on all other session types. When NFI is red with negative
TSB (genuine fatigue), tempo sessions now correctly return a recovery
workout instead of a scaled tempo session.

Fixes the failing test: 'generates a recovery workout when NFI is red
and fatigued'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SilverSprint
repository.description: SilverSprint is a "Neural-First" training platform. It operates on the principle that for aging sprinters, Central Nervous System (CNS) recovery is the primary bottleneck for performance. The application must automate the detection of neural fatigue and provide actionable "Go/No-Go" training recommendations.

commit.message:
fix: use SprintTrainingPlan.buildContext() for mock training plan

Replaced hand-crafted mockTrainingPlan (plan: []) with a call to
SprintTrainingPlan.buildContext(), which populates the full 12-week
plan array. The empty plan[] caused TrainingPlanPanel to crash on
context.plan[planWeek - 1] access, resulting in a blank demo page.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[BMC] Move platform_tests skip marks to correct conditional mark file (#23523)

Summary: Move 11 BMC-related platform_tests/ skip entries from tests_mark_conditions.yaml to tests_mark_conditions_platform_tests.yaml to avoid duplicate key conflicts.

When BMC skip marks for platform_tests/ paths are defined in the main tests_mark_conditions.yaml, they can conflict with entries in tests_mark_conditions_platform_tests.yaml that define conditions for the same test paths (e.g., platform_tests/broadcom/test_ser.py). This PR moves all BMC platform_tests skip marks to the correct file.

Signed-off-by: Jing Kan <jika@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: 主题色改用色轮任意选色，修复颜色不生效问题

- 废弃 CSS class 方案，改用 element.style.setProperty 直接写入
  CSS 变量（内联样式优先级最高，不再被 :root 覆盖）
- AccentColor 类型改为 hex 字符串，默认值 #FF7F43
- 添加 hexToHsl 转换函数
- 暗色模式自动提亮 accent 以保持可见性
- 切换亮暗模式时自动重新应用 accent（修复暗色模式下颜色失效）
- UI：6 个预设色 + 原生 input[type=color] 色轮任意选色
- 移除多余 CSS accent 类

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: harden email security, add validation, and Playwright email tests

- Rename NEXT_PUBLIC_EMAIL_* → EMAIL_* to keep SMTP credentials server-only
- Add EMAIL_REGEX validation and escapeHtml() XSS protection to /api/contact
- Add in-memory rate limiter (1hr per email) to /api/contact
- Move email env vars from public to private schema in config.ts
- Remove email credential exports from config.client.ts
- Use module-level singleton transporter in contact and subscribe routes
- Add tableEnsured flag in /api/subscribe to avoid DDL on every invocation
- Remove unused resend dependency
- Add src/tests/email.test.ts: Playwright API tests covering template server
  health, contact validation, rate limiting, and subscribe flow
- Add test and test:email scripts to package.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow /model command from main group (Telegram owner)

WhatsApp uses is_from_me for owner detection, but Telegram messages
are never from_me since the bot is a separate account. Now also
allows /model from the main group (isMain), which is the owner's
direct chat.

commit.message:
[bgp/agg]: Add BGP aggregate address test cases for Dual-Stack Concurrent Operation (#23547)

Add Test Group 9 covering dual-stack (IPv4 + IPv6) aggregate-address
concurrent operation:

- TC 9.1 test_dual_stack_simultaneous_aggregates: Both IPv4 and IPv6
  aggregates configured and active simultaneously via a single GCU patch
- TC 9.2 test_dual_stack_bbr_toggle: Both address families with
  bbr-required=true respond correctly to BBR state changes
- TC 9.3 test_dual_stack_mixed_bbr_required: Mixed bbr-required flags
  across address families — only the bbr-required aggregate is withdrawn
  when BBR is disabled

Route injection uses ExaBGP from M0 downstream neighbors.  Aggregate
route verification polls the DUT BGP table via vtysh.  All verify helpers
use wait_until polling to handle bgpcfgd processing delays.

Signed-off-by: Shixi Zhang <shixz@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: amplihack-rs
repository.description: Rust core runtime for amplihack deterministic infrastructure (hooks + CLI)

commit.message:
docs: retcon documentation for phases 3–6 (agent system, eval, hive, generator) (#146)

Add comprehensive documentation describing the finished state of all
remaining phases as specification for implementation:

Concepts (5 new):
- agent-lifecycle.md — Agent state machine, session management, OODA loop
- domain-agents.md — Teaching, code review, meeting synthesis agents
- eval-framework.md — Progressive L1–L12 evaluation, graders, self-improvement
- hive-orchestration.md — Multi-agent swarm deployment and event system
- agent-generator.md — Four-stage goal-to-agent pipeline

Reference (7 new):
- agent-core-api.md — Full API for amplihack-agent-core crate
- domain-agents-api.md — Full API for amplihack-domain-agents crate
- agent-eval-api.md — Full API for amplihack-agent-eval crate
- hive-api.md — Full API for amplihack-hive crate
- agent-generator-api.md — Full API for amplihack-agent-generator crate
- memory-extended-api.md — Extended memory API (facade, manager, kuzu, eval)
- agent-configuration.md — Complete config reference (env vars, TOML, precedence)

How-to (4 new):
- create-custom-agent.md — Build a domain agent with memory and eval
- run-agent-evaluations.md — Evaluate agents across progressive levels
- deploy-hive-swarm.md — Deploy 100-agent hive on Azure Container Apps
- generate-agent-from-goal.md — Create agents from natural-language goals

Updated docs/index.md and README.md with links to all new documentation.

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devy.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[bgp/agg]: Add BGP aggregate-address functional params and BBR dynamics test cases (#23379)

* Skip test_telemetry_queue_buffer_cnt when BUFFER_QUEUE table is absent (#23115)

On certain topologies (e.g., vlab-08), the DUT config_db does not contain
a BUFFER_QUEUE table, causing test_telemetry_queue_buffer_cnt to crash
with KeyError: 'BUFFER_QUEUE' at line 166.

Add a guard to gracefully skip the test when BUFFER_QUEUE is not present
or empty in the config_db, consistent with the existing skip patterns
already used later in the same test function.

Signed-off-by: Storm Liang <stormliang@microsoft.com>

* [vm_set]: Add fallback default for VM_targets when VMs are not required (#23361)

When ptf_imagename is 'docker-keysight-api-server', vm_required is set
to false, which skips the block that defines VM_targets. However,
remove_topo.yml and add_topo.yml reference VM_targets unconditionally,
causing 'VM_targets is undefined' errors during topology teardown.

Add a top-level fallback that sets VM_targets to an empty list when it
is not defined.

* Fix import error in test_bgp_aggregate_address_resilience.py

BGP_AGGREGATE_ADDRESS, PLACEHOLDER_PREFIX, dump_db, and
gcu_add_placeholder_aggregate are defined in bgp_aggregate_helpers.py,
not in test_bgp_aggregate_address.py. The resilience test was importing
them from the wrong module, causing ImportError.

Fix by importing these symbols from bgp_aggregate_helpers directly,
and only importing AGGR_V4/AGGR_V6 from test_bgp_aggregate_address.

commit.message:
feat: add date range controls to Dashboard trend charts (#149)

Introduces DateRangeFilter component with preset buttons (7d, 30d, 90d,
All time) and filterSeriesFileByDateRange utility for filtering series
data by ISO timestamp ranges.

Wired into Dashboard via DashboardToolbar — all trend charts, overview
cards, regression detection, and monitor metrics respect the selected
date range. Default is 'all' for backward compatibility.

Includes 11 tests for presetToDateRange and filterSeriesFileByDateRange.

Closes #55

commit.message:
Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Michaux Sean Kelley <michauxkelley@gmail.com>

feat: add RunDetail as a reusable run inspection surface (#148)

Introduces RunDetail component that renders a complete run view:
- RunMetadataBar: run ID, timestamp, commit, ref, benchmark/metric counts
- RunnerContextPanel: collapsible OS/CPU/memory/polling info from monitor
- MetricSnapshotCard: per-metric cards with series values and ranges
- Comparison section: VerdictBanner + ComparisonSummaryTable when provided
- Monitor metrics partitioned into a secondary collapsible section

The component supports two modes:
1. Preloaded: pass detail={runDetailView} directly
2. Fetch-on-demand: pass source + runId for automatic loading

Designed for reuse across RunDashboard, CompetitiveDashboard, and
custom metric explorers.

Includes 9 tests and CSS for all new elements.

Closes #82

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor index creation in setup_db.py to data-driven loop

- Replace 42 individual cursor.execute() calls with an _INDEXES
  tuple-of-tuples and a compact loop.
- Add PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON after connecting.
- No functional change to the generated SQL.

commit.message:
feat(bundle): add packager, distributor, update_manager modules (#144)

Add three new modules ported from the Python bundle_generator:

- packager.rs: Packager trait + FileSystemPackager for .tar.gz, .zip,
  directory, and UVX bundle creation with SHA-256 checksums
- distributor.rs: Distributor for GitHub release creation (via gh CLI)
  and local filesystem distribution with manifest generation
- update_manager.rs: UpdateManager for version checking, customisation
  detection, backup/rollback, and template-based bundle updates

All modules registered in mod.rs, use existing error.rs/models.rs types,
stay under 400 lines, and include 36 tests in tests.rs (95 total).
Clippy warning count unchanged at 42.

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devy.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
backend: implement project-aware turn storage (#32)

* feat: add project-aware turn storage

Add a project-aware turn ingestion endpoint, JSON repository persistence, agent_id canonical request handling, and direct backend docs/tests for issue #26.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden turn storage identifiers

Reject path traversal in project-aware JSON storage, normalize blank agent identifiers to unassigned semantics, and align the design doc with the implemented endpoint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
